audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for frogs, gnats/lice, swarm, Pharaoh's partial concessions, distinction of Goshen, and hardening.

verses.restored: YHWH said to Moses, Come to Pharaoh, and say to him, Thus says YHWH: Send my people away, so they may serve/worship me.
But if you refuse to send, look, I myself will strike all your border with frogs.
The Nile will swarm with frogs; they will go up and come into your house, your bedroom, your bed, house of your servants, among your people, into your ovens and kneading bowls.
Upon you, upon your people, and upon all your servants the frogs will go up.
YHWH said to Moses, Say to Aaron, Stretch your hand with your staff over rivers, canals, and pools, and bring up frogs upon land of Egypt.
Aaron stretched his hand over waters of Egypt, and frogs came up and covered land of Egypt.
The magicians did so with their secret arts and brought up frogs upon land of Egypt.
Pharaoh called Moses and Aaron and said, Plead with YHWH to remove frogs from me and from my people, and I will send the people, so they may sacrifice to YHWH.
Moses said to Pharaoh, Have honor over me: for when shall I plead for you, your servants, and your people, to cut off frogs from you and your houses, only in Nile they will remain?
He said, Tomorrow. Moses said, According to your word, so that you may know there is none like YHWH our Divine.
The frogs shall turn aside from you, from your houses, from your servants, and from your people; only in Nile they shall remain.
Moses and Aaron went out from Pharaoh, and Moses cried to YHWH concerning the frogs he had set upon Pharaoh.
YHWH did according to word of Moses; frogs died from houses, courtyards, and fields.
They piled them in heaps and heaps, and the land stank.
But Pharaoh saw there was relief, and he made his heart heavy and did not listen to them, as YHWH had spoken.
YHWH said to Moses, Say to Aaron, Stretch your staff and strike dust of earth, and it shall become gnats/lice in all land of Egypt.
They did so; Aaron stretched his hand with his staff and struck dust of earth, and gnats/lice were on human and beast; all dust of earth became gnats/lice in all land of Egypt.
The magicians did so with their secret arts to bring out gnats/lice, but they were not able. And gnats/lice were on human and beast.
The magicians said to Pharaoh, This is finger of Divine. But Pharaoh's heart was strong/hardened, and he did not listen to them, as YHWH had spoken.
YHWH said to Moses, Rise early in morning and stand before Pharaoh; look, he goes out to the water. Say to him, Thus says YHWH: Send my people away, so they may serve/worship me.
For if you do not send my people away, look, I send swarm upon you, your servants, your people, and your houses; houses of Egypt will be full of swarm, and even ground on which they are.
But on that day I will set apart land of Goshen, where my people stand, so no swarm will be there, so that you may know that I, YHWH, am in midst of land.
I will place distinction between my people and your people; tomorrow this sign will happen.
YHWH did so; a heavy swarm came into house of Pharaoh and house of his servants and in all land of Egypt; the land was ruined because of swarm.
Pharaoh called Moses and Aaron and said, Go, sacrifice to your Divine in the land.

Moses said, It is not established/right to do so, for we will sacrifice to YHWH our Divine what is abomination to Egypt. Look, if we sacrifice what is abomination to Egypt before their eyes, will they not stone us?
A three-day journey into wilderness we will go, and we will sacrifice to YHWH our Divine, as he says to us.
Pharaoh said, I myself will send you, and you shall sacrifice to YHWH your Divine in wilderness; only do not go very far. Plead for me.

Moses said, Look, I go out from you and will plead to YHWH, and the swarm will turn aside from Pharaoh, his servants, and his people tomorrow; only let Pharaoh not act deceitfully again by not sending the people to sacrifice to YHWH.
Moses went out from Pharaoh and pleaded to YHWH.
YHWH did according to word of Moses and turned aside the swarm from Pharaoh, his servants, and his people; not one remained.
But Pharaoh made his heart heavy this time also, and he did not send the people.

translation_notes: Frog and insect signs continue creation-disruption imagery.
Magicians recognize 'finger of Divine' when imitation fails.
Pharaoh's concessions remain control tactics: worship in land, do not go far, plead for me.
safety_note: The signs confront Pharaoh's repeated refusal and control tactics. Do not read them as Divine cruelty detached from liberation context.
